title: hieracium pilosella subsp. pachyanthoides
intro: Hieracium pilosella subsp. pachyanthoides, commonly known as Hawkweed, is a subspecies of flowering plant belonging to the dandelion family (Asteraceae). The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its high content of sesquiterpene lactones and flavonoids. The medicinal properties of Hawkweed have been found to have various health benefits, including reducing pain and inflammation, as well as exhibiting antimicrobial and antioxidant activities. Hawkweed can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in herbal remedies to treat wounds, skin conditions, and digestive issues.
